original_hypothesis.title: forceTransfer Bypasses Transaction Fee Mechanism Entirely
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: forceTransfer() calls _update() directly without going through transferSanity(), completely bypassing the _payTxFee mechanism. A compromised or malicious ADMIN can move tokens between arbitrary addresses without paying any transaction fees, depriving the protocol of fee revenue on potentially large transfers.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While forceTransfer is designed for compliance (seizure), the complete absence of fee logic means it can be used as a fee-free transfer channel. If ADMIN routinely uses forceTransfer instead of regular transfers for non-compliance purposes, the protocol loses all fee revenue on those transfers.

proof_of_signal.pattern: forceTransfer calls adminSanity then _update directly; never calls transferSanity or _payTxFee

preconditions: ADMIN uses forceTransfer for non-compliance transfers

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: forceTransfer(A, B, 1000000): 0 fees paid. transfer(B, 1000000): (1000000 * _txfeeRate) / 10000 fees paid
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: ADMIN routes transfers through forceTransfer to avoid fees

economic_questions: Is fee exemption on forceTransfer intentional by design?
What is the total fee revenue lost if ADMIN uses forceTransfer regularly?
validation_requirements: Test: forceTransfer with _txfeeRate > 0 - confirm no fee deduction
Compare with regular transfer fee deduction on same amount
Review design documentation for intended fee behavior on forceTransfer

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Fee savings = transferAmount * feeRate / 10000 per use
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Up to feeRate% savings per transfer

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time with active fee rate

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: forceTransfer execution path vs transfer path

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
